column5: I feel like the initial pitch for the event hinted that raids would be the highest level of content to be expected, so I was not prepared for an inferno or colosseum. I know there were the stored drops we could in the mean time but that largely felt meaningless (which I understand is partly by design). But overall I think we used maybe 2 stored drops out of all of the ones we had. I know a lot of my other team member also prioritized the stored drops as well which added to my frustration of struggling to contribute in a meaningful way. Just for context sake, I was on team 2
column6: The early couple of days where we had tiles I was able to assist with
column8: The rest of my frustration of the event really is just time zones as everyone would be active while I was at work and then off once I was off work everyone had already retired for the evening. I also  know that partly this is my own social anxiety for not asking for people once I was able to play. So really, overall a great event, I did enjoy trying to get drops and seeing how the teams raced for tiles.

column4: The bot is singlehandedly the best upgrade for these events, makes them much more smoother!
column5:
Positive: I liked the flexibility for drops required on some tiles compared to the prior event, not too task hunting requirements either.

Negative : Repeating tiles with the same drop requirement, I loved the mixed in "get X kc as a team" of certain bosses of the bomb event. 
I'm not a fan of tiles with really hard challenging content like Inferno, it makes the team relly to much on a very specific few individuals whilst the rest are mostly benched and unable to get moving and it puts all the pressure on they key member(s) that can clear the content or the team gets soft locked. I think giving an alternative like the team needs to get 10 fire capes with a maximum of 2 per players or something would be a good point of interest to look at. I also think the Collo unique was a great addition as that doesn't require a full on clear, so maybe similarly for inferno it could be have 5 players reach wave X, encourage learning like the Collo tile for uniques
column6: When we were 4 manning and massing nex for a day and a half, finally managed to clear it and rolled into a Huey tile, then suddenly we got like 10 more people to jump in and come clear the tile!
column8:
I think it could be fun to switch up the tiles or clearing requirements! I thought the cannonballs and plank drops challenge was super unique and a great idea, I would love to see more of that and find a way to incorporate it in normal tiles, so we don't end up always going for purples or similar content. 

Use more of the gain X kc :3 where was the moleeee?! 

I very much so love these pvm events driven by strategy and time, but I hope we soon enough get events with a different rule set. In essence, these last few events (albeit fantastic!) were all about focusing on 1 tile at a time and some banked loot on the plus side of maybe it will be useful (strategy), I kind of miss a good old race event with multiple options, like the Hexes last wear where we had the strategy to decide where to go and form our areas, but all the whilst having different options directly impacting the game instead on relying on store drops that can suddenly turn the tables around if you hit 5 stored tiles in a row.

Love y'all and appreciate everything, reminder that those are simply MY thoughts ☺️

column4: Think it was excellently done, although the bot channel was a little laggy
column5: Being able to store drops was great as everyone could be contributing in some way even if unable to work on the current tile

Felt like some of the rush that you feel when you have an event and you get lucky was hindered by requiring multiple uniques at a boss. In this instance.. the battleship tiles were meant to be a little challenging and made sense with the time invested. Hitting water and it's a easier tile meant to get quickly and take your next shot, but when getting a spoon, and realizing you're still not done really slows the event down. While it was needed with manual reffing for Mario party, now that the bot is automated I would love to see a fast pace designed into the event. Something balanced like having most tiles be a one and done of the tile, and an occasional "unlucky" tile (ensure you can't keep hitting these multiple uniques at bandos tiles) and the tiles that progress your team being exactly how we did ship tiles ( gp values raids fun content etc.) 

Also would like to see inferno and col tiles that require completions to be challenges until we have more people not afraid to go spend 2 hrs clearing to prevent people from farming stored tiles. 

As a suggestion for stored tiles.. personally I don't think we want stored tiles, I think having pity mechanics in the tile (x many shards... X many vials of blood...) should be a better use of your time. We should push people to engage in the content or preparation work rather than actively farming  something. Having people unable to engage in a tile they should go tureal skip for a good boss task coming up in front of your tile.. and hold the task, then go grind entry mode, or contribute to the pity mechanic, maybe the tob tile has a blood shard = 50 vials of blood. We can be creative for those that may have a thieving level but haven't learned the raid, or choose to do entry. We should keep teams focused on the same task

column4: Honestly, the bot worked great for me
column5:
There were some tasks that were clearly aimed at lategame pvm players (the Inferno, Coloseum) that midgame players on the team struggled to meaningfully contribute towards. Now, midgame players could do other bosses to try and bank drops, but the limitations on storing drops as well as uncertainty around future tasks meant that those efforts didn't feel like they mattered in most cases.

One tile I really liked was the 150m worth of boss/raid uniques as a team. That tile let everyone contribute by running content that was in their gear/skill bracket.

Obviously, team balance is difficult, and the game was close for a bit, but Team 1 had significantly more hours contributed than Team 2 by Wise Old Man metrics. Going into the final day, Team 1 had a pretty clear advantage. The event felt over before it ended because Team 1 had a solid lead that Team 2 would have needed a crazy amount of spooning across multiple bosses/raids to overcome. Learning the Inferno isn't something I can do in a few hours/days, especially with every attempt taking so long before succeeding/failing, and that was reflected in Team 2 being stuck for 6 hours on the Inferno tile while Lyreth cleared it 3 consecutive times, all the while Team 1 was landing more hits. Coloseum was better, but only because you can get uniques from it without needing to clear it, and so people who could not clear it could still contribute.

It also felt bad to get the first sink in the game, because there were some tasks that were more difficult if you had sunk a ship, and Team 1 deliberately left ships unsunk to take advantage of this mechanic, which does not exist in the regular battleship game. The goal is to sink ships, and abusing rubber-banding mechanics designed to keep the event close felt against the spirit of the event and game. Maybe it didn't factor at all, but it was something that weighed on me during the event.
column6: Bossing with JJ Disco is always fun, even when people plank, because he posts the receipts. I had to work, but seeing pictures of the team massing Huey also looked fun.
